jokes.text: Is anybody worried about dirt on their food here? You know, these buses come by. Everybody makes such a big deal about eating outside. - I find it disgusting. - It's al fresco.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about outdoor dining and dirt
jokes.punchline: Someone responds with pretentious 'al fresco' as if that solves the hygiene issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry neurosis about mundane things, well-executed contrast between his practical concern and pretentious response

jokes.text: You're wearing gloves? It's the middle of summer. - No, my baseball glove. - Oh, baseball.
jokes.setup: Larry assumes Jeff is wearing dress gloves in summer
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a baseball glove, making Larry's concern absurd
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that works through Larry's misassumption, economical delivery

jokes.text: Are you not listening anything I've ever told you?
jokes.setup: Larry clearly hasn't been paying attention to Jeff's softball activities
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated response reveals this is a pattern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's self-absorption and Jeff's frustration

jokes.text: You I can understand. You've been married a long time. You could care less about what he does. - You're absolutely correct. - But we've just started dating. And when you first start dating you always do things that are distasteful. - That's part of the process.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining relationship dynamics about attending partner's events
jokes.punchline: Susie immediately agreeing she doesn't care what Jeff does
jokes.explanation: Sharp relationship observation with Susie's brutal honesty as perfect punchline

jokes.text: That fish, to be honest, don't look so good. I only chose it because I knew we were gonna split it. Look, you brought the fish into it. I'm saying no. - That's completely unethical.
jokes.setup: They agreed to split fish and chicken, but Larry wants to keep the better dish
jokes.punchline: Jeff calling Larry's food selfishness 'completely unethical'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry selfishness escalated to mock-serious ethical territory

jokes.text: this is why he's a good manager. You should be thankful he's unethical. He's unscrupulous.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about Jeff's unethical behavior
jokes.punchline: Susie defending unethical behavior as a managerial asset
jokes.explanation: Susie's twisted logic turning character flaws into virtues

jokes.text: Can I try it? - Nope. - Just a little taste? - Nope, you made a mistake.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to taste Jeff's food after refusing to split
jokes.punchline: Jeff's absolute refusal, treating Larry like a child
jokes.explanation: Jeff's petty revenge delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: No, you're only saying you learned a lesson because I said I learned a lesson. That's where you're wrong. I'm just sharing. I know for a fact you did not learn a lesson. - It's a coincidence.
jokes.setup: Larry claims to have learned a lesson, Jeff says he did too
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid accusation that Jeff is copying him
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry paranoia about the most mundane interaction

jokes.text: Good luck at your wiffle ball game or whatever. - Softball.
jokes.setup: Jennifer dismissively calling it wiffle ball
jokes.punchline: Jeff's correction to softball, showing her disinterest
jokes.explanation: Jennifer's dismissive attitude captured in one word choice

jokes.text: She likes you, which is a shocking thing to me. - She might.
jokes.setup: Jeff expressing shock that someone likes Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's modest 'she might' as if it's uncertain
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Lar. Lar. Larry! What happened? Where'd you go?
jokes.setup: Larry clearly zoned out during Susie's request
jokes.punchline: Susie's escalating attempts to get his attention
jokes.explanation: Larry's attention deficit shown through Susie's exasperation

jokes.text: To tell you the truth, whenever I see a woman who's happy, she's married. And whenever I see a man who's happy, he's single. - Mathematics of that is confusing, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Larry's observation about happiness and relationship status
jokes.punchline: Jeff pointing out the logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor with mathematical impossibility as punchline

jokes.text: When I was a kid I had a traumatic incident involving Mr. Softee.
jokes.setup: Hearing Mr. Softee music triggers Larry
jokes.punchline: Dramatic buildup to 'traumatic incident involving Mr. Softee'
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for childhood trauma involving ice cream truck - absurd but believable for Larry

jokes.text: That's pretty pretty pretty good.
jokes.setup: Strip poker context
jokes.punchline: Larry's signature catchphrase in inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Iconic Larry catchphrase deployed in strip poker flashback

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing with my daughter? Your vagina is showing. Oh my God. He's got more pubes on his fucking head.
jokes.setup: Larry getting caught in strip poker by girl's father
jokes.punchline: Father's crude observations about Larry's nudity
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment with crude humor about Larry's appearance

jokes.text: I feel like if she had taken the top off my whole life would have been different.
jokes.setup: Recounting strip poker trauma to therapist
jokes.punchline: Larry attributing life trajectory to missing one glimpse
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to blame major life issues on minor childhood moments

jokes.text: You know, before I came out today I think about I'm like the Steinbrenner. And I think, 'What would Steinbrenner say right now?' He would say that when you put on this uniform with my name on it, there is no substitute for winning!
jokes.setup: Manager comparing himself to legendary Yankees owner
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top speech for amateur softball team
jokes.explanation: Delusional manager taking softball way too seriously

jokes.text: we will grab them and we will fuck their sisters in the cunt! Are you listening? Bring it in. Come on. - Fuck these people! - Fuck these people!
jokes.setup: Motivational speech escalating
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate sexual threat as team motivation
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that goes too far, relies on crude escalation

jokes.text: Fuck their sisters in the cunt? Who talks that way?
jokes.setup: Manager's inappropriate speech
jokes.punchline: Larry's perfectly reasonable bewilderment
jokes.explanation: Larry as voice of reason against shocking crudeness, perfect timing

jokes.text: I got distracted by the Softee Truck. - By the what? - What a douche. What a douche.
jokes.setup: Larry makes crucial error in championship game
jokes.punchline: Admits distraction by ice cream truck music
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier setup, Larry's neurosis costing his team championship

jokes.text: You fucking bucknered it. Why is Buckner on my team?
jokes.setup: Larry makes error letting ball through legs
jokes.punchline: Comparing to famous Bill Buckner World Series error
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for later Buckner meeting, turns baseball legend into verb

jokes.text: Let me just say this, okay? On behalf of all Caucasians, I apologize. I apologize for the Caucasian race.
jokes.setup: Leon can't get into building due to doorman
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top racial apology
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate attempt at racial solidarity

jokes.text: I have noticed that white people revere black people in glasses, go out of their way to do stuff for them. If a black man with glasses goes up for a job against a white man, glasses gets the job. No glasses, no job.
jokes.setup: Larry offering Leon advice about getting respect
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous theory about glasses as racial equalizer
jokes.explanation: Provocative racial observation that's absurd enough to work

jokes.text: I get a fucking tingle in my Johnson when a motherfucker tells me he's about to get some ass.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions seeing Jennifer tonight
jokes.punchline: Leon's graphic expression of excitement
jokes.explanation: Leon's crude enthusiasm characteristic but relies on shock value

jokes.text: You didn't fix my car because I made an error? What are you, 12 years old? What are you, 90 years old?
jokes.setup: Yari didn't fix car because Larry made error
jokes.punchline: Larry's age insults missing the mark completely
jokes.explanation: Larry's confused age-based insults show his flustered state

jokes.text: You own 12 t-shirts.
jokes.setup: Yari claiming to be like Steinbrenner who 'owns this team'
jokes.punchline: Larry's deflating reality check about softball uniform ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Yari's grandiose comparison with brutal economy

jokes.text: Buckner, fuck you. Go Buckner yourself. Go! Faster!
jokes.setup: Yari firing Larry from the team
jokes.punchline: Using 'Buckner' as verb meaning to screw up
jokes.explanation: Creative use of Buckner as verb, building on earlier callback setup

jokes.text: We're good. Can you believe that? I'm gonna get me a whole new class of bitches with these on.
jokes.setup: Leon wearing glasses gets them into sold-out event
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude celebration of glasses theory working
jokes.explanation: Payoff to glasses setup with Leon's characteristic crudeness

jokes.text: This is better than anything the civil rights leaders have ever come up with. You're goddamn right. I have overcome, God damn it.
jokes.setup: Glasses working to get respect
jokes.punchline: Leon comparing glasses to civil rights movement
jokes.explanation: Bold comparison of trivial glasses theory to civil rights, works through absurd confidence

jokes.text: You know when you were a kid walking with your mother through Bohack's, you saw your teacher and you had to realize she didn't live in school? You understand? Larry, I don't live in school either. - I have no idea what you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Therapist making elaborate analogy about seeing him outside office
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete confusion at the analogy
jokes.explanation: Therapist's over-elaborate analogy falling flat with Larry

jokes.text: She called me Mr. Softee.
jokes.setup: Larry telling therapist about sexual dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Woman's cruel nickname connecting to his ice cream truck phobia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback connecting childhood trauma to adult dysfunction, perfect cruel irony

jokes.text: I had a client once, he was a rock guitarist. He played for the band, what was the name of it? Grand Funk Railroad. I don't want to reveal his name because-- I already told you he played for Grand Funk Railroad. You can just look it up. It's Mark Farner.
jokes.setup: Therapist claiming confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing specific patient details anyway
jokes.explanation: Therapist's complete failure at maintaining confidentiality

jokes.text: Of course yours happened in the world series in front of millions of people, but nevertheless a ball went through my legs and cost us the game.
jokes.setup: Larry comparing his softball error to Buckner's World Series error
jokes.punchline: Larry equating local softball to World Series
jokes.explanation: Larry's narcissistic comparison of his minor error to historic sports failure

jokes.text: He's the first one to wear that since Hitler, isn't he? You know what? I think that's true.
jokes.setup: Discussing Michael Jordan's Hitler mustache commercial
jokes.punchline: Buckner agreeing Jordan is first since Hitler to wear the look
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Hitler mustache with unexpected agreement

jokes.text: Hey, Buckner, you suck. Nice catch. What jerks, huh? Oh my God. How do you put up with that? You just get used to it.
jokes.setup: Buckner getting heckled on street
jokes.punchline: Buckner's resigned acceptance after decades
jokes.explanation: Sad reality of Buckner's life, shows impact of sports infamy

jokes.text: When a Jewish person dies you need to have 10 men in a room to say a prayer. - It'll take 10 minutes tops. I got my friend here. - He could come along. It's a free lunch.
jokes.setup: Emergency need for a minyan
jokes.punchline: The increasingly desperate sales pitch including free lunch
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation with the free lunch incentive, typical of awkward social recruiting.

jokes.text: Hopefully there's an afterlife. I'll keep my fingers crossed. You never know. Crazier things, right?
jokes.setup: Larry offering condolences at a funeral
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate casual speculation about the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - turning funeral consolation into casual philosophical speculation with complete social tone-deafness.

jokes.text: He's a big Red Sox fan and he can't be in the same room with Bill Buckner. I'll have to ask you to please leave.
jokes.setup: Buckner attending Jewish funeral as favor
jokes.punchline: Being kicked out of funeral for baseball-related trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Buckner hatred to religious ceremony

jokes.text: Don't let the door go through your legs on the way out, Buckner.
jokes.setup: Buckner being kicked out of funeral
jokes.punchline: Cruel baseball pun even at funeral
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cruel pun on Buckner's error, even mourners can't resist

jokes.text: I hope there is no afterlife. I hope there's no afterlife.
jokes.setup: Larry offended by treatment at funeral
jokes.punchline: Wishing no afterlife out of spite
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry pettiness - theological revenge for funeral slight

jokes.text: Hey, Buck, catch. What?! What are you doing? - What the fuck?
jokes.setup: Larry tossing the signed Mookie Wilson ball to Buckner
jokes.punchline: Buckner dropping it, recreating his famous error
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the episode's baseball themes - Buckner recreating his famous error with the exact player's signature who caused it. Brilliant structure and payoff.

jokes.text: Hey, Buck, catch. What?! What are you doing? It was a horseshit throw. No, it was a horseshit catch also.
jokes.setup: Larry throws valuable signed ball to Buckner
jokes.punchline: Buckner drops it, both blame each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Buckner drops another ball, fulfilling expectations with perfect irony

jokes.text: It was Mookie Wilson, Buckner! Yeah. What'd Mookie do? All he hit was a shitty ground ball.
jokes.setup: Mookie Wilson ball is ruined
jokes.punchline: Buckner dismissing Mookie's famous hit as 'shitty ground ball'
jokes.explanation: Buckner's defensive dismissal of the hit that destroyed his reputation

jokes.text: You're not allowed to just say, 'Hey, let's have sex.' You've got to go around it... Like you're doing something else so you fool the woman. It's like a magic trick. It's sleight of hand.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his seduction strategy
jokes.punchline: Comparing romance to magic tricks and deception
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about dating rituals with Larry's characteristic over-analysis of social conventions.

jokes.text: The dried pineapple is destroying mixed nuts.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing snack preferences
jokes.punchline: His dramatic assessment of pineapple as destructive to mixed nuts
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry neurosis about food combinations with perfect dramatic overstatement.

jokes.text: Like for golf, when I have a bad round, my next round is fantastic because I don't care anymore. I hit the low point. Like, I hit the low point the last time. And now I know that I stink. And when I feel I stink I'm good.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to convince Jennifer to come upstairs
jokes.punchline: Bizarre theory that being bad at sex will make him good
jokes.explanation: Larry's convoluted logic for why sexual failure leads to success

jokes.text: Bad news, I have no dip. I am so sorry. I am completely dipless.
jokes.setup: Building up the importance of chip and dip pairing
jokes.punchline: The dramatic revelation and the word 'dipless'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the invented word 'dipless' and Larry's typical over-apologizing for minor issues.

jokes.text: That's a remarkably idiotic thing that you just said. But you know, the fact that you're wearing glasses, I perceive it as a little less idiotic than I normally would.
jokes.setup: Leon making stupid comment while wearing glasses
jokes.punchline: Larry admitting glasses theory actually works on him too
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Larry falling victim to his own theory

jokes.text: This shit is a moving dildo. This chair is a fuck machine. Man cannot compete with machinery. You're damn right. She was coming downstairs.
jokes.setup: Jennifer was satisfied by vibrating car seat
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude analysis and pun on 'coming'
jokes.explanation: Leon's crude but logical conclusion about car seat satisfaction

jokes.text: I don't want to reveal who he was, but he did direct 'Star Wars.' I would never say that. I merely alluded to the fact that he was a well-known director. You might as well call him George Lucas.
jokes.setup: Therapist claiming confidentiality again
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing it's George Lucas while denying he revealed it
jokes.explanation: Second major confidentiality breach, even more blatant than the first

jokes.text: He tore the bill up, threw it in the garbage. Done. What? You're kidding. No, I'm not kidding. the glasses, baby. Amazing.
jokes.setup: Leon confronting therapist with glasses on
jokes.punchline: Glasses theory working perfectly to get bill canceled
jokes.explanation: Major payoff to glasses theory - actually solves Larry's problem

jokes.text: No, I stole it.
jokes.setup: Larry amazed Leon got signed ball
jokes.punchline: Leon's casual admission of theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon moment - casual criminality delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh my God! I can't believe it! Unbelievable. I've never seen anything like that in my life. - Nice catch, Bill.
jokes.setup: Baby dropped from burning building
jokes.punchline: Bill Buckner makes the crucial catch, redeeming himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect series climax - Buckner redeems himself by catching a baby, ultimate callback payoff
